content: Diagnosis requires tissue confirmation. Evaluation: transvaginal ultrasound (assesses ovarian morphology, solid components, ascites); CT abdomen/pelvis (disease extent, resectability); chest imaging for metastases. CA-125 baseline and for monitoring; HE4 (human epididymis protein 4) sometimes used; ROMA score combines CA-125 and HE4. Preoperative risk assessment (RMI, IOTA) guides referral to gynecologic oncologist. Definitive diagnosis usually from surgical specimen; biopsy reserved for unresectable disease before neoadjuvant chemotherapy. Staging: FIGO surgical staging system - Stage I (confined to ovaries), II (pelvic extension), III (peritoneal metastases outside pelvis and/or retroperitoneal nodes), IV (distant metastases including pleural effusion with positive cytology, parenchymal metastases). Genetic testing: germline BRCA1/2 testing recommended for all epithelial ovarian cancer patients; tumor testing for BRCA and HRD status guides PARP inhibitor use. Genetic counseling for positive results.
